title: AI o11y — Loadgen Activity (what k6 is driving right now)

panels.title: 📡 Overview — is k6 actually generating traffic right now?
How fast is k6 driving chats? (req/sec)
panels.description: Rate of Sigil generation log lines from llm-gateway (one per chat). Best proxy for live k6 throughput. Red below 0.1/sec means loadgen is essentially idle.

panels.title: How many use cases are firing?
panels.description: Distinct ai_o11y.usecase values seen in llm-gateway logs in the last 5 minutes. Green at 18+ — registry has 22 scenarios so anything well below means traffic is too thin to keep them all lit.

panels.title: How many users are active?
panels.description: Distinct user_id (email) values seen in llm-gateway logs in the last 5 minutes. Green at 20+ — the centerpiece users (Tim/Mara/Jordan/Priya/Eric) should always be present.

targets.expr: count(sum by (user_id) (count_over_time({container="llm-gateway"} |~ "sigil generation:" | pattern `<_>sigil generation: <jsonline>` | line_format "{{.jsonline}}" | json | user_id=~".*@.*" [5m])))

panels.title: 💰 What is loadgen burning per hour? (USD/hr)
panels.description: Total gen_ai cost rate × 3600. Almost all of this is Claude — the 80% Ollama traffic is essentially free. Yellow above $5/hr, red above $25/hr.

panels.title: 📊 Mix breakdown — which scenarios and personas are running?
Which use cases are getting hit? (chats per use_case)
panels.description: Total chats per use_case in the last 5 minutes. Should mirror the baseline.js weight table at the bottom — data-theft-tim (8) and hiring-discrimination-risk (5) lead.

panels.title: 🔥 Which personas are driving the chats?
panels.description: Total chats per persona in the last 5 minutes. Centerpiece personas (Tim L, Mara, Jordan, Priya, Eric) should dominate — if they don't, baseline.js weights need a look.

targets.expr: topk(25, sum by (user_id) (count_over_time({container="llm-gateway"} |~ "sigil generation:" | pattern `<_>sigil generation: <jsonline>` | line_format "{{.jsonline}}" | json | user_id=~".*@.*" [5m])))

panels.title: 📈 Over time — how is the mix changing, and where is traffic landing?
Use-case traffic over time (top 12 by rate)
panels.description: Per-use_case chats/sec over the dashboard window. Use this to watch a scenario ramp, dip, or die out after you tweak baseline.js weights and restart k6.

panels.title: 📦 Where is traffic landing? (specialist mix)
panels.description: Donut split of chats by destination specialist. Sanity check that sb-router and nc-chatbot are fanning out to their respective sub-specialists, not getting stuck on a single one.

panels.title: 🤖 Model routing + freshness — are providers and scenarios alive?
🤖 Ollama vs Claude — is the 80/20 split holding?
panels.description: Donut of gen_ai.system share. Target ratio is set by LOADGEN_CLAUDE_FRACTION (default 0.10–0.20). If Claude is dominating, you'll see it here before the cost stat panel turns red.

panels.title: ❄ Which scenarios have gone cold? (hits in last 30m)
panels.description: Per-use_case hit count over the last 30 minutes, sorted high-to-low. Red row = zero hits — usually a baseline.js weight at 0, a k6 pod restart, or stale routing keywords in sb-router.

panels.title: 📋 Scenario config — what is baseline.js asking k6 to drive?

options.content:
## Current loadgen mix (from `baseline.js`)

Top 20 weighted scenarios — the offender archetypes (Tim/Mara/Jordan/Priya/Eric) are intentionally heavy so the centerpiece panels stay lit.

| Weight | Use case | Persona | App |
|---:|---|---|---|
| **8** | data-theft-tim | u-tim-l | supportbot |
| **5** | hiring-discrimination-risk | u-hr-recruiter | supportbot |
| 4 | prompt-injection | u-eric-bad | supportbot |
| 4 | prompt-injection-llm01 | u-eric-bad | supportbot |
| 4 | normal-support (HR) | u-emp-norm-1 | supportbot |
| 4 | normal-support (IT) | u-emp-norm-2 | supportbot |
| 4 | normal-shopping | u-bob-sales | neoncart |
| 4 | normal-shopping | u-alice-eng | neoncart |
| 4 | **mice-rca** (centerpiece) | u-customer-mice | neoncart |
| 4 | **email-cascade** | u-mara-chen | supportbot |
| 4 | **cost-anomaly-per-user** | u-priya-research | supportbot |
| 4 | **confidential-disclosure** | u-jordan-finance | supportbot |
| 3 | sensitive-data-leaks | u-emp-test-pii | supportbot |
| 3 | refund-policy-compliance | u-shopper-refund | neoncart |
| 3 | prompt-injection | u-shopper-injector | neoncart |
| 3 | normal-support (hiring) | u-hr-recruiter | supportbot |
| 3 | normal-support (expense) | u-emp-norm-3 | supportbot |
| 3 | normal-shopping | u-carol-mktg | neoncart |
| 3 | model-winner | u-shopper-gift | neoncart |
| 3 | customer-frustration | u-frustrated | neoncart |
| 3 | cost-anomaly-per-user | u-priya-research | neoncart |
| 3 | brand-voice-drift | u-norm-2 | neoncart |
| 3 | bad-question-askers | u-emp-rude | supportbot |

...plus 16 more @ weight 2 (token-spikes, toxicity, pii-echo, tool-call-runaway, policy-circumvention, outlier-users, quality-trend, hallucination-product-price, and 8 normal-support buckets).

**Total scenarios:** 47 across 22 use cases and 30 personas.

## Model routing

- **80%** of chats: Ollama (default `llama3.2:1b`, chart-supplied)
- **20%** of chats: Claude — uniformly across `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-opus-4-7`
- Controlled by `LOADGEN_CLAUDE_FRACTION` env (default `0.10`)

## To tune the mix

Edit [`/workspace/observibelity/registry/_generated/scenarios/baseline.js`](https://github.com/stephenwagner-grafana/observibelity/blob/main/registry/_generated/scenarios/baseline.js), then:

```bash
helm upgrade observibelity /workspace/observibelity \
  -f /workspace/observibelity/values-deploy.yaml \
  --namespace observibelity --reuse-values --timeout 5m
kubectl rollout restart -n observibelity deployment/k6-traffic
```

## Related dashboards

- [Cost & spend](/d/ai-obs-cost) — total $/hr by provider / model / user
- [NeonCart app](/d/ai-obs-app-neoncart) — per-use-case panels for nc-* scenarios
- [SupportBot app](/d/ai-obs-app-supportbot) — per-use-case panels for sb-* scenarios
- [Demo landing](/d/ai-obs-landing) — full demo agenda
